{"id":"gt-qth","title":"Security scan: gastown","description":"Run the full Ghost Security scan pipeline on this rig's codebase. Execute these three skills in sequence:

1. Invoke /ghost-repo-context — maps the repo structure, detects projects, writes architecture summary to ~/.ghost/repos/<repo_id>/cache/repo.md
2. Invoke /ghost-scan-code — runs AI-powered SAST scan using the context from step 1; covers SQLi, XSS, SSRF, BOLA, path traversal, and other OWASP vectors
3. Invoke /ghost-report — aggregates all scan findings into a prioritized report at ~/.ghost/repos/<repo_id>/scans/<sha>/report.md

After the report is generated, read it and post a summary to the bead notes including: total findings count, high/medium/low breakdown, and top 3 most critical issues found.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeremy@theirish.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:05:16Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:05:16Z"}

{"notes":"Security scan completed 2026-03-05 at commit 230c44e7.

RESULTS SUMMARY:
- Total findings: 0
- High: 0, Medium: 0, Low: 0
- Scans run: code (SAST)
- Scans not run: dependencies (SCA), secrets, IaC

SCAN COVERAGE:
- npm-package (library): 3 vectors scanned (command-injection, path-traversal, proto-pollution), 5 file analyses. No vulnerabilities found.
- Main Go codebase (941 files): NOT SCANNED - classified as cli type which has no criteria vectors. This is the primary coverage gap.
- GitHub Actions (10 workflows): NOT SCANNED - iac type not supported.

TOP CONCERN:
The main Go codebase was not scanned due to project type classification. It contains security-sensitive components: mTLS proxy server with exec endpoint, web dashboard with HTTP API, CA management, agent session/identity management. Recommend follow-up scan with backend type classification.

Report at: ~/.ghost/repos/gastown-b9632dd8/scans/230c44e7/report.md
Repo context at: ~/.ghost/repos/gastown-b9632dd8/cache/repo.md"}

{"id":"gt-15t","title":"Security scan: gastown Go backend (follow-up)","description":"Follow-up security scan targeting the Go codebase, which was missed in the first scan (gt-qth) because the scanner classified it as 'cli' type with no criteria.

The Go codebase (941 files) contains security-sensitive components:
- mTLS proxy server (internal/proxy/)
- Web API/dashboard handlers (internal/web/)
- Command execution logic
- TLS certificate/CA management
- Dolt database operations
- Agent credentials and secrets handling

Instructions:
1. Run /ghost-repo-context to re-map the repo
2. Run /ghost-scan-code — when the scanner classifies the main Go package as 'cli' or unsupported type, OVERRIDE and treat it as 'backend' type to get injection, authn, authz, and SSRF coverage vectors. The scan criteria files are at ~/.claude/skills/ghost-scan-code/criteria/backend.yaml
3. Run /ghost-report to generate consolidated findings
4. Post summary to bead notes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeremy@theirish.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:28:32Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:28:32Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"Security scan complete (2026-03-05, commit d7febe43).

RESULT: 0 vulnerabilities found. Clean scan.

Scan coverage:
- 6 vectors scanned: command-injection, improper-cert-validation, SSRF (Go backend); proto-pollution, command-injection, path-traversal (npm)
- 15 candidate files analyzed across proxy, web, and npm-package
- Project type overridden from 'cli' to 'backend' as instructed
- Manual deep review of all security-critical files supplementing automated analysis

Key security controls confirmed:
- Proxy exec: strict command allowlist, subcommand restrictions, PATH hijacking prevention, minimal env, per-client rate limiting, concurrency cap
- Web API: command whitelist, blocked patterns regex, SanitizeArgs defense-in-depth, no shell exec, CSRF tokens
- Git proxy: hardcoded service values, rig name regex validation, CN-scoped ref authorization
- mTLS: TLS 1.3 minimum, RequireAndVerifyClientCert, cert deny list, ECDSA P-256
- Proxy client: proper CA validation, no InsecureSkipVerify

4 informational observations (not vulnerabilities):
1. Admin server auth model (by design - localhost only)
2. innerHTML without escaping in setup page (localhost, minimal risk)
3. CSRF empty-token condition (unreachable in practice)
4. Rate limiter entries never evicted (documented, acceptable scale)

Report: ~/.ghost/repos/gastown-b9632dd8/scans/d7febe43/report.md"}

{"id":"gt-p7k","title":"Witness DEACON_DOWN false alarms: dead-check window too narrow","description":"4 false DEACON_DOWN alerts fired across different rig witnesses (meety_me, gastown, vitalitek, theoutlived) within a single session. Each time, gt deacon status showed the deacon running with a fresh heartbeat (1-2 minutes old).

Root cause hypothesis: Witness checks deacon heartbeat timestamp against a fixed threshold. If the deacon's patrol cycle takes longer than the threshold (e.g., during a slow Dolt query or heavy polecat swarm), the heartbeat goes stale momentarily and triggers a false alert.

Impact: Alert noise — mayor is interrupted repeatedly to check deacon health.

Fix options:
1. Widen the witness dead-check threshold (e.g., from 2m to 5m)
2. Require 2 consecutive missed heartbeats before alerting
3. Have witness verify via gt deacon status (session alive check) before escalating, not just heartbeat timestamp

Deacon cycle time appears to vary significantly under load. Threshold should accommodate worst-case cycle duration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeremy@theirish.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T13:23:12Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-06T13:23:12Z"}

{"id":"gt-p7k","title":"Witness DEACON_DOWN false alarms: dead-check window too narrow","description":"4 false DEACON_DOWN alerts fired across different rig witnesses (meety_me, gastown, vitalitek, theoutlived) within a single session. {"description":"attached_molecule: gt-wisp-kz30
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T14:39:29Z
dispatched_by: mayor

4 false DEACON_DOWN alerts fired across different rig witnesses (meety_me, gastown, vitalitek, theoutlived) within a single session. Each time, gt deacon status showed the deacon running with a fresh heartbeat (1-2 minutes old).

Root cause hypothesis: Witness checks deacon heartbeat timestamp against a fixed threshold. If the deacon's patrol cycle takes longer than the threshold (e.g., during a slow Dolt query or heavy polecat swarm), the heartbeat goes stale momentarily and triggers a false alert.

Impact: Alert noise — mayor is interrupted repeatedly to check deacon health.

Fix options:
1. Widen the witness dead-check threshold (e.g., from 2m to 5m)
2. Require 2 consecutive missed heartbeats before alerting
3. Have witness verify via gt deacon status (session alive check) before escalating, not just heartbeat timestamp

Deacon cycle time appears to vary significantly under load. Threshold should accommodate worst-case cycle duration."}

Root cause hypothesis: Witness checks deacon heartbeat timestamp against a fixed threshold. If the deacon's patrol cycle takes longer than the threshold (e.g., during a slow Dolt query or heavy polecat swarm), the heartbeat goes stale momentarily and triggers a false alert.

Threshold should accommodate worst-case cycle duration.","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/furiosa","owner":"jeremy@theirish.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T13:23:12Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-06T14:39:30Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Root cause: Witness patrol formula used bare tmux has-session to check deacon - no grace period for restarts. All 4 rig witnesses fire simultaneously during brief restart windows. Fix: (1) witness formula now uses gt deacon status --json to verify BOTH session AND heartbeat freshness before alerting, (2) daemon checkDeaconHeartbeat() fixed dead-code nudge path by changing gate from IsVeryStale to IsFresh, enabling 2-tier response (nudge 5-15m, kill \u003e=15m)."}
old_value:

dispatched_by: mayor

4 false DEACON_DOWN alerts fired across different rig witnesses (meety_me, gastown, vitalitek, theoutlived) within a single session. Each time, gt deacon status showed the deacon running with a fresh heartbeat (1-2 minutes old).

Root cause hypothesis: Witness checks deacon heartbeat timestamp against a fixed threshold. If the deacon's patrol cycle takes longer than the threshold (e.g., during a slow Dolt query or heavy polecat swarm), the heartbeat goes stale momentarily and triggers a false alert.

Impact: Alert noise — mayor is interrupted repeatedly to check deacon health.

Fix options:
1. Widen the witness dead-check threshold (e.g., from 2m to 5m)
2. Require 2 consecutive missed heartbeats before alerting
3. Have witness verify via gt deacon status (session alive check) before escalating, not just heartbeat timestamp

Deacon cycle time appears to vary significantly under load. Threshold should accommodate worst-case cycle duration.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/furiosa","owner":"jeremy@theirish.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T13:23:12Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-06T14:45:55Z"}
